perturbation.original_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. ARC acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of ------------------ the covenants set forth in this Agreement will cause irreparable injury and damage to Belcan for which Belcan would have no adequate remedy at law, and ARC further agrees that in the event of said breach, Belcan, without having to prove damages, shall be entitled to an immediate restraining order and injunction to prevent such violation or continued violation, and to all costs and expenses, including Belcan's reasonable attorney fees, in addition to any other remedies to which Belcan may be entitled at law or equity.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Assignment. This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the ----------- benefit of Belcan, its successors and assigns. 5. Injunctive Relief. ARC acknowledges and agrees that a breach of any of ------------------ the covenants set forth in this Agreement will cause irreparable injury and damage to Belcan for which Belcan would have no adequate remedy at law, and ARC further agrees that in the event of said breach, Belcan, without having to prove damages, shall be entitled to an immediate restraining order and injunction to prevent such violation or continued violation, and to all costs and expenses, including Belcan's reasonable attorney fees, in addition to any other remedies to which Belcan may be entitled at law or equity.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the 'Injunctive Relief' clause to the 'Assignment' section buries a critical legal remedy within a standard contractual provision. This makes it less likely to be noticed or understood, potentially hindering Belcan's ability to quickly seek injunctive relief in case of a breach. Injunctive relief is time sensitive, so obfuscating its location could prejudice Belcan.
perturbation.law_explanation: Ohio Revised Code § 2727.02 outlines the grounds for granting injunctive relief. By obscuring the Injunctive Relief clause within the 'Assignment' section, its prominence is diminished, making it more difficult to enforce and understand the specific circumstances under which Belcan can seek immediate injunctive relief. This reduces the immediacy of the remedy, potentially conflicting with the spirit of the statute which allows for prompt action to prevent irreparable harm.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Section 2727.02 - Ohio Revised Code | Ohio Laws Skip to main content Back To Top Top The Legislative Service Commission staff updates the Revised Code on an ongoing basis, as it completes its act review of enacted legislation. Updates may be slower during some times of the year, depending on the volume of enacted legislation. Section 2727.02 | Causes for an injunction. Ohio Revised Code / Title 27 Courts-General Provisions-Special Remedies / Chapter 2727 Injunctions Effective: October 1, 1953 Latest Legislation: House Bill 1 - 100th General Assembly PDF: Download Authenticated PDF A temporary order may be granted restraining an act when it appears by the petition that the plaintiff is entitled to the relief demanded, and such relief, or any part of it, consists in restraining the commission or continuance of such act, the commission or continuance of which, during the litigation, would produce great or irreparable injury to the plaintiff, or when, during the litigation, it appears that the defendant is doing, threatens or is about to do, or is procuring or permitting to be done, such act in violation of the plaintiff's rights respecting the subject of the action, and tending to render the judgment ineffectual. Available Versions of this Section October 1, 1953 – House Bill 1 - 100th General Assembly [ View October 1, 1953 Version ]
perturbation.justification: The modified contract text contradicts legal principles by structurally obscuring a critical remedy. The relocation of the 'Injunctive Relief' clause diminishes its prominence and accessibility. Ohio Revised Code § 2727.02, as shown in Scraped Law Snippet 1, allows for a temporary order restraining an act when it appears the plaintiff is entitled to relief and the act would produce great or irreparable injury. By burying the Injunctive Relief clause, the contract makes it more difficult for Belcan to quickly and effectively assert their right to this remedy, potentially hindering their ability to prevent irreparable harm and conflicting with the spirit of the statute that allows for prompt action, which is a structural flaw.
